messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: you've probably already internalized it

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: it's important to understand how value is generated and that's important to understanding which units are good in what situation

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: I have no knowledge of math to internalize. Obviously I have an intuitive understanding that having hp now instead of later lets you make better turns.

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: But that is as simple as understanding that a build 2 tarsiers is worse than a build 1 tarsier cause a build 1 tarsier does an extra damage.

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: It's not a complicated or mathy concept.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: ok but why is damage important?

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: why does tarsier have more value than drones?

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: why build drones before tarsier if swords are worth more than drones

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Absorb makes early attackers useless.

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: I can explain everything without math and nothing with math.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: once you go over the absorb barrier, why ever build drones

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: and why do drones suck?

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Its like saying starcraft is a math heavy game and saying "but then why is it bad to float resources" if anyone claims otherwise.

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Drones suck cause if you buy too many you lose.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: why do you lose when you buy too many?

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Don't know don't care.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: more drones = more money = more defense

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Of course you need to be capable of understanding consequences of mistakes and seeing patterns in the results of actions.

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: But that isn't math.

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: I'm not saying Prismata is easy or that people can be masters out of the box.

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: i do believe you need to understand how the numbers interact with each other at least

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Well I don't know how the numbers interact with each other.

messages.author: jamesa7171

[2018-05-04 20:00] jamesa7171: (n.b. i don't personally care either way about the monument stuff and that is not an attack on it)

[2018-05-04 20:05] masn6811 (Masne98888): where strong puzzle solvers secretly spend a week micro-optimizng every puzzle, and then editing the conditions so that the solution is super difficult and unique
[2018-05-04 20:05] masn6811 (Masne98888): then you give the puzzle to vivid-e-sports and granular-gaming
[2018-05-04 20:06] masn6811 (Masne98888): and stream it as both sides try to solve it first

[2018-05-04 20:07] supergreyman (Greyman): Makes me wonder how many players out there are puzzle lovers.
[2018-05-04 20:08] supergreyman (Greyman): I'm personally ok with it, but I can't stand hours of doing one puzzle. 😛
[2018-05-04 20:08] jamesa7171: i'm definitely one - i would probably join in on the monument stuff if i was actually good enough, optimizing puzzle solutions is one of my favourite things to do
[2018-05-04 20:09] masn6811 (Masne98888): but if people are willing to go through the trouble to host tournaments, i think team puzzle could fill a pretty big niche. and i remember how cool it was to have everyone, weak and strong players alike just work together on the puzzle
[2018-05-04 20:09] mtanzer: yeah optimizing solutions is so fun
[2018-05-04 20:09] masn6811 (Masne98888): imagine if someone new plays the game, and within two months they are collabing with top players trying to solve a puzzle
[2018-05-04 20:09] mtanzer: for me at least.  Like I enjoy playing the game a lot too but ladder certainly has its ups and downs.  with single-player stuff it's always positive, you're always improving.  the con being it's just less rewarding I guess

[2018-05-04 20:10] masn6811 (Masne98888): but even if dbelange (the top player at the time) beat it first, but used silvertail's opening, i think silvertail would have had a blast
[2018-05-04 20:11] masn6811 (Masne98888): reminds me of carrying in the team round so hard during ARML. that was fun too
[2018-05-04 20:11] mtanzer: btw james you mentioned making a sheet to keep track of the monument stuff.  that's something I could easily do but as long as it's only me and masn doing it I just haven't bothered

--- Thread (46 messages, 2018-05-04) ---
[2018-05-04 20:15] mtanzer: yeah the feel of arml is so different
[2018-05-04 20:15] masn6811 (Masne98888): but it's so much more entertaining when there's so many people watching. and i don't feel at a disadvantage, because everyone else is in the same position
[2018-05-04 20:19] masn6811 (Masne98888): but i think the puzzle race is a suggestion @Elyot should hear. like maybe start it one month after release, and do it monthly? so the new players can watch and join in. maybe i don't know what people like, but i think players might enjoy being able to actively participate in a team event
[2018-05-04 20:20] supergreyman (Greyman): Just start the contest once Elyot releases a puzzle pack. 😉
[2018-05-04 20:20] jamesa7171: i have a feeling the response will be "maybe one day, but about 500,000,000 things are ahead of it on the priority list"
[2018-05-04 20:22] mtanzer: yeah I think if you really want something like that to happen the best chance is to organize it yourself using what's already in the game
[2018-05-04 20:22] mtanzer: I'm not expecting any nontrivial changes to the client any time soon, lunarch's got their hands full already
[2018-05-04 20:22] masn6811 (Masne98888): if we had the ability to create and share the puzzles, then i think there might be people in the community willing to do the work
[2018-05-04 20:23] mtanzer: although maybe some sort of simple leaderboard for single-player stuff using the data that's already there is doable?
[2018-05-04 20:23] mtanzer: I think having support for making your own puzzles would be awesome but falls in the category of things that won't happen any time soon
[2018-05-04 20:25] jamesa7171: if you guys are serious about continuing with monument challenges / other puzzles of similar natures you're likely going to want more serious leaderboards anyway, so i'm not sure a simple lunarch leaderboard would accomplish much
[2018-05-04 20:25] masn6811 (Masne98888): like even if making it is somewhat unwieldy. like maybe you need to make a .json to make a puzzle, but then you can just upload it to the client, and you'd be able to playtest it smoothly, and share it to others, i think that might be enough
[2018-05-04 20:26] masn6811 (Masne98888): and each uploaded puzzle would have it's own ID, and you can just keep it secret for 'internal testing', and release it to everyone and game day
[2018-05-04 20:26] masn6811 (Masne98888): and all the teams would have their own streams about them trying to figure out the strategy
[2018-05-04 20:27] jamesa7171: and sniping each others' streams? :Lul:
[2018-05-04 20:27] mtanzer: I think having a simple leaderboard could potentially get more people interested
[2018-05-04 20:28] supergreyman (Greyman): And each team will secretly employ spies on one another. 👀
[2018-05-04 20:28] jamesa7171: i will say that if such a team event ever does happen, there should be a "team everyone else" so the public can race against the teams to add to the fun
[2018-05-04 20:30] jamesa7171: from my experience running a puzzle optimization leaderboard for another game, just the presence of the leaderboard itself both makes people interested in it and gives the pursuit more credibility in general (to players who optimize as well as those that don't)
[2018-05-04 20:31] masn6811 (Masne98888): they can do stream delay if they want
[2018-05-04 20:32] supergreyman (Greyman): Wouldn't that encourage smurfs to try to beat the hardest puzzle in a few seconds?
[2018-05-04 20:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): i feel like an in-game monument leaderboard could be cool enough
[2018-05-04 20:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): i don't think time-based leaderboards are a good idea, for that reason
[2018-05-04 20:33] mtanzer: james what other game was that?
[2018-05-04 20:36] jamesa7171: chip's challenge (the original game, the sequel, and the four "best of" community level packs, comprising 945 levels in total with multiple leaderboards per level depending on rule version)
[2018-05-04 20:37] jamesa7171: the community is nowhere near as active as it was in the mid 90s when the windows version came pre-installed on a bunch of PCs, but it's still active almost 30 years after game release
[2018-05-04 20:37] mtanzer: chip's challenge lol that's actually the first video game I owned
